raw_data.details: The scheme “Development and Dissemination of e-Content” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.
raw_data.benefits: The high-quality e-learning materials from online education providers (platforms such as Byju's and Vedantu) are made available to students of classes 9 to 12 through free bilingual online and offline lectures in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a student from Uttarakhand.
raw_data.application_process: OnlineStudents can access certain e-learning materials by visiting the USERC website, [https://www.userc.in](https://www.userc.in). However, to access the full course, they can download the Vidyasaar app from the Google Play Store and register their mobile number free of charge. In schools without internet access, USERC provides a device to the school, enabling students to access the entire course offline.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityUttarakhandDevelopment and Dissemination of e-ContentDigital LearningE-learningOnline EducationStudentDetailsThe scheme “Development and Dissemination of e-Content” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms. BenefitsThe high-quality e-learning materials from online education providers (platforms such as Byju's and Vedantu) are made available to students of classes 9 to 12 through free bilingual online and offline lectures in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English. EligibilityThe applicant must be a student from Uttarakhand. Application ProcessOnlineStudents can access certain e-learning materials by visiting the USERC website, [https://www.userc.in](https://www.userc.in). However, to access the full course, they can download the Vidyasaar app from the Google Play Store and register their mobile number free of charge. In schools without internet access, USERC provides a device to the school, enabling students to access the entire course offline. Documents RequiredMobile NumberFrequently Asked QuestionsWhich classes are covered? Students of classes 9th to 12th are eligible. Which subjects are available? The subject like Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. are available. Is the content multilingual? Yes, it is available in both Hindi and English. How can students access the content? Through the Vidya Saar mobile app or offline devices in schools. Is the app free to use? Yes, the Vidya Saar app is completely free. What if a student has no internet access? Offline devices with preloaded content are provided to schools. Can students access content from home? Yes, if they have internet and a mobile device with the Vidya Saar app. Is registration mandatory? Yes, students must register with a valid mobile number on the app. Are there any charges for lectures? No, the lectures are provided free of cost. Sources And References[Guidelines (Page No. 118)](https://uk.gov.in/department92/library_file/file-04-12-2023-06-02-23.pdf)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: Which classes are covered?
faqs.answer: Students of classes 9th to 12th are eligible.

faqs.question: Which subjects are available?
faqs.answer: The subject like Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. are available.

faqs.question: Is the content multilingual?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is available in both Hindi and English.

faqs.question: How can students access the content?
faqs.answer: Through the Vidya Saar mobile app or offline devices in schools.

faqs.question: Is the app free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vidya Saar app is completely free.

faqs.question: What if a student has no internet access?
faqs.answer: Offline devices with preloaded content are provided to schools.

faqs.question: Can students access content from home?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they have internet and a mobile device with the Vidya Saar app.

faqs.question: Is registration mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, students must register with a valid mobile number on the app.

faqs.question: Are there any charges for lectures?
faqs.answer: No, the lectures are provided free of cost.
